questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: There is a circuit diagram in which capacitors, resistors \& battery are given. Then choose the correct option (s) (Assume initally all the capacitors are uncharged)
options: At steady state charge on capacitor A is zero
At steady state charge on capacitor $B$ is zero
At steady state charge on capacitor B is $40 \mu \mathrm{C}$
Electric current in $2 \Omega$ resistor is 5 A
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a circuit with a constant current source of $I_S=2 \mathrm{~mA}$ with a capacitor of $2 \mu \mathrm{~F}$ and resistor of $5 \mathrm{k} \Omega$ (as shown in figure). At $t=0$ the switch is closed. $I_C \& I_R$ denotes current in the capacitor \& resistor branch respectively and $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{C}}$ represents potential difference across the capacitor. Choose the correct graph(s).
questions.solution:
At $t=0$, capacitor act a short circuit $\&$ current in resistor branch $=0$. 
At $\mathrm{t}=\infty, \mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{R}}=2 \mathrm{~mA} ; \mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{C}}=0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Three identical plates of area $A$ are arranged as shown. A charge $+Q$ is given to plate (2). Then,
options: $\text { charge }+2 Q / 3 \text { appears on plate }$
$\text { charge }-2 \mathrm{Q} / 3 \text { appears on plate } 1$
$\text { charge }-Q / 3 \text { appears on plate } 3$
$\text { charge on two faces of plate } 2 \text { are } 2 Q / 3 \text { and } Q / 3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Select correct alternative/s If both plates of parallel plate capacitor are given positive charge as shown in figure and at $\mathrm{t}=0$ switch is closed :
options: current in resistance ' $R$ ' will flow from $A$ to $B$
electric field intensity between plate of capacitor will decrease continuously
current in the circuit at $t$ is $\frac{Q}{2 R C} e^{-t / R C}$
total heat developed in the circuit long after switch is closed is $\frac{Q^2}{4 C}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A capacitor of capacitance ' $C$ ' is connected to a battery of emf $\varepsilon$ as shown. After full charging a dielectric of same size as that of capacitor \& dielectric constant k is inserted. Then choose the INCORRECT statement(s). (Capacitor remains always connected to battery)
options: electric field between plates of capacitor remains same
charge on capacitor increases to $2 \mathrm{kC} \varepsilon$
energy stored in capacitor decreases
electric field between plates of capacitor increases
questions.solution: P.D. same \& electric field same Charge will be $\mathrm{kC} \varepsilon$ Energy will be $1 / 2 \mathrm{kC} \varepsilon^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Following operation can be performed on a capacitor :
X - connect the capacitor to a battery of emf E.
Y - disconnect the battery.
Z - reconnect the battery with polarity reversed.
W - insert a dielectric slab in the capacitor.
options: In XYZ (perform $X$, then $Y$, then $Z$ ) the stored electric energy remains unchanged and no thermal energy is developed.
The charge appearing on the capacitor is greater after the action XWY than after the action XYW.
The electric energy stored in the capacitor is greater after the action WXY than after the action XYW.
The electric field in the capacitor after the action XW is the same as that after WX.
questions.solution:
(B) First C increases
(C) $\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{CV}^2 \& \frac{1}{2} \frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{\mathrm{KC}}$
(D) $\mathrm{E}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{d}}$ in both case potential difference across plate is same.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A parallel plate capacitor with plate area $A$ and separation d has charge $Q$. A slab of dielectric constant k is inserted in space between the plates almost completely fills the space. If $\mathrm{E}_0$ and $\mathrm{C}_0$ be the electric field and capacitance before inserting the slab, then
options: the electric field after inserting the slab is $\frac{E_0}{k}$
the capacitance after inserting the slab is $\mathrm{k}  \mathrm{C}_0$
the induced charge on the slab is $\mathrm{Q}\left(1-\frac{1}{\mathrm{k}}\right)$
the energy stored in the capacitor becomes $\frac{\mathrm{U}_0}{\mathrm{k}}, \mathrm{U}_0$ being the energy of the capacitor before inserting the slab
questions.solution:
Q - Remain same 
Capacitance become KC
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A parallel plates capacitor is made of square conducting plates of side $a$ and the separation between plates is d. The capacitor is connected with battery of emf $V$ volt as shown in the figure. There is a dielectric slab of dimension a $\times \mathrm{a} \times \mathrm{d}$ with dielectric constant $k$. At $t=0$, dielectric slab is given velocity $v_0$ towards capacitor as shown in the figure. (Neglect the effect of gravity and electrostatic force acting on the dielectric when dielectric is out side of capacitor. Also ignore any type frictional force acting on the dielectric during its motion) let the $x$ be the length dielectric inside the capacitor at $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{t}$ sec. $\left[\ell_0 \gg \mathrm{a}\right]$
options: Motion of dielectric slab is periodic but not simple harmonic motion
Motion of dielectric slab is simple harmonic motion
At any time, the slope of graph of total energy verses $x$ is twice the slope of graph of potential energy verses $x$.
The value of maximum energy stored in the system is $\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_0^2+\frac{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{a}^2 \mathrm{~V}^2}{2 \mathrm{~d}}(2 \mathrm{k}-1)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & C=\frac{\varepsilon_0(a-x) a}{d}+\frac{\varepsilon_0 x a k}{d} \\ & =\frac{\varepsilon_0 a[a-x+k x]}{d} \\ & =\frac{\varepsilon_0 a}{d}[a+(k-1) x]\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{U}=\frac{\varepsilon_0 a \mathrm{~V}^2}{2 \mathrm{~d}}[\mathrm{a}+(\mathrm{k}-1) \mathrm{x}] \\ & \mathrm{F}=\frac{\mathrm{dU}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{aV}^2(\mathrm{k}-1)}{2 \mathrm{~d}} \\ & \mathrm{v}^2=\mathrm{v}_0^2+2 \mathrm{Ax}=\mathrm{v}_0^2+\frac{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{aV}^2(\mathrm{k}-1)}{\mathrm{md}} \mathrm{x}\end{aligned}$ $E=\frac{1}{2} m v_0^2+\frac{\varepsilon_0 a^2(k-1) x}{2 d}+\frac{\varepsilon_0 a V^2}{2 d}[a+(k-1) x]$ $\mathrm{E}_{\max }=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_0^2+\frac{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{a}^2 \mathrm{~V}^2(2 \mathrm{k}-1)}{2 \mathrm{~d}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A capacitor of capacitance C is connected to two voltmeters A and $\mathrm{B} . \mathrm{A}$ is an ideal voltmeter having infinite resistance, while $B$ has resistance $R$. The capacitor is uncharged and then the switch $S$ is closed at $\mathrm{t}=0$,
options: Readings of $B$ and A will be $\varepsilon$ and zero at $t=0$
During time interval $(0 \leq \mathrm{t} \leq \infty)$ readings of B and A are changing
Reading of A and B will be equal at $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{RC} \ln 2$
None of these
questions.solution:
Potential across A \& B should same.
$\frac{\mathrm{V}_0}{2}=\mathrm{V}_0\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{\mathrm{t}}{\mathrm{RC}}}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
